scenario: A retailer migrating 320 microservices to Camunda 8.8 must wire up OAuth client credentials for each Spring Boot worker fleet. The platform team needs to confirm the exact documented property key whose value carries the client identifier used when requesting an access token from the OAuth authorization server.
question: Which property key holds the client ID used when requesting an access token from the OAuth authorization server in a Camunda 8.8 starter?

options.text: camunda.client.auth.clientId is the property key whose value is the client identifier used when requesting an access token from the OAuth authorization server
camunda.client.auth.client-id is the property key whose value is the client identifier used when requesting an access token from the OAuth authorization server
camunda.client.auth.oauth-client is the property key whose value is the client identifier used when requesting an access token from the OAuth authorization server
zeebe.client.cloud.client-id is the property key whose value is the client identifier used when requesting an access token from the OAuth authorization server

a.text: Incorrect. The Spring Boot starter uses kebab-case for property keys; the documented form is camunda.client.auth.client-id, bound to environment variable CAMUNDA_CLIENT_AUTH_CLIENTID.

b.text: Correct. camunda.client.auth.client-id is the documented Spring Boot property whose value is sent as the client identifier when requesting an access token from the OAuth authorization server.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no oauth-client key under camunda.client.auth; the documented client identifier property is named client-id under camunda.client.auth in the starter.

d.text: Incorrect. zeebe.client.cloud.client-id belongs to the legacy Zeebe-era starter; the Camunda 8.8 starter exposes the unified camunda.client.auth.client-id key for OAuth.

explanation: The Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot starter documents camunda.client.auth.client-id as the property whose value is sent as the OAuth client identifier when the Camunda client requests an access token. The corresponding environment variable is CAMUNDA_CLIENT_AUTH_CLIENTID.
